title: Quality Compounding

content.question: What are you letting be 'good enough' that should be excellent?
sop: List the last 5 things you shipped or delivered.
Rate the quality of each honestly, 1-10 (see calculation, page 4), not against competitors but against your own ceiling.
For anything under an 8, identify exactly what corner was cut, time, budget, attention.
Rate how visible your best work actually is, 1-10 (see calculation, page 4), great work nobody sees doesn't attract opportunity.
Pick one recurring deliverable and raise its floor this month, then make the improvement visible.
questions: What are you letting be 'good enough' that should be excellent?
What's the last piece of work you shipped that you'd be embarrassed to have a dream client see?
Are your best opportunities finding you because of your standards, or are you still chasing all of them?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10: Quality, Visibility. Quality 9 times Visibility 4 equals 36. Higher means excellent work is reaching people who could act on it; lower means either the work isn't excellent yet, or it's excellent and invisible, both cap your opportunity.
content.closing_statement: Opportunity finds the standard, not the hustle.

caption:
Principle Nº 176: Quality Compounding

Better output attracts better opportunities.

Ask yourself: What are you letting be 'good enough' that should be excellent?
